text:
Once upon a moonlit evening, nestled deep within the lush woods, a small owl perched upon a gnarled oak branch. She loved owl pellets for a breakfast treat. On this particular morning, her owl pellet counter was unusually low—an owl pellet deserves a hefty breakfast after all that owl-night owl-time.

As she nibbled on her hard work, a tiny creature flapped through the night sky, bouncing off her feathers. It was a tiny hedgehog, hedgehogs love owl pellets. The hedgehog eyed the owl pellet hungrily.

With a fluttery snout, the tiny hedgehog whispered, “Little owl pellet, you’re quite plump.”

The owl pellet remained unmoving. With a giggle that could quiet a midsummer night storm, the hedgehog fluttered closer. “Hmmm,” she pondered aloud, tasting the scent of her favorite snack. “A bit too peppery?”

The little hedgehog nibbled thoughtfully, tasting the mysterious essence.
